children.label: i
children.text: existing policies and practices affecting the science and technology reinvention laboratories to improve the mission effectiveness of such laboratories;

children.label: ii
children.text: new initiatives proposed by the science and technology reinvention laboratories; and

children.label: iii
children.text: new interpretations of existing provisions of law that would enhance the ability of a director of a science and technology reinvention laboratory to manage the laboratory and discharge the mission of the laboratory;

children.label: B
children.text: to support implementation of current and future initiatives affecting the science and technology reinvention laboratories; and

children.label: C
children.text: to conduct assessments or data analysis on the effectiveness of the authorities granted to the science and technology reinvention laboratories and such other issues as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
The program carried out pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be known as the “Laboratory Quality Enhancement Program”.
A panel on personnel, workforce development, and talent management.
A panel on facilities, equipment, and infrastructure.
A panel on research strategy, technology transfer, and industry and university partnerships.
A panel on governance and oversight processes.

children.label: A
children.text: laboratories and research centers of the Army, Navy, and Air Force;

children.label: B
children.text: appropriate Defense Agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other entities as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: at least one member from each of the science and technology reinvention laboratories; and

children.label: B
children.text: such other members as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
The chairperson of each panel established pursuant to subsection (a) shall be selected by the members of the respective panel.

Each panel, in coordination with the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering, shall transmit to the Science and Technology Executive Committee of the Department of Defense such information or findings on topics requiring decision or approval as the panel considers appropriate.

The Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering, acting under the guidance of the Secretary, shall issue regulations regarding the meaning, scope, implementation, and applicability of any provision of a statute relating to a science and technology reinvention laboratory.

In interpreting or defining under paragraph (1), the Under Secretary shall, to the degree practicable, emphasize providing the maximum operational flexibility to the directors of the science and technology reinvention laboratories to discharge the missions of their laboratories.

In interpreting or defining under paragraph (1), the Under Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, consult and coordinate with the secretaries of the military departments and such other agencies or entities as the Under Secretary considers relevant on any proposed revision to regulations under paragraph (1).
In interpreting or defining under paragraph (1), the Under Secretary shall seek recommendations from the panel described in subsection (b)(4).

children.heading: .—Science and Technology Reinvention Laboratory Defined
children.label: f .—Science and Technology Reinvention Laboratory Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “science and technology reinvention laboratory” means a Department of Defense laboratory designated as a Department of Defense science and technology reinvention laboratory under .section 4121 of this title
